mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate below 3% signals accommodative monetary policy stance that reduces borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, lowering equity risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5%) when FEDFUNDS < 3%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with expanding housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, signaling economic momentum that supports equity valuations and produces positive 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 352138M and HOUST at 2159K as of 2010-07
regime_hint: early-cycle expansion with synchronized demand recovery
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of instances when both RSAFS and HOUST show month-over-month growth, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals weakening manufacturing activity and potential economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as markets reprrice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth combined with declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals robust labor market tightening that increases wage pressures and inflation expectations, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth > 200K month-over-month AND UNRATE decline > 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 132843K and UNRATE at 5.4% as of 2010-08
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market tightening with inflation risk
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 48% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of PAYEMS and UNRATE is not statistically significant (interaction p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerating above 2.5% year-over-year signals rising inflation expectations that force the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, raising discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: inflation acceleration regime requiring policy response
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1.5 million annualized units signals deteriorating housing demand and construction sector weakness, which reduces multiplier effects on employment and consumer spending, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 48% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth combined with rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals synchronized supply-side and labor-market expansion that supports corporate profit margins and productivity gains, producing positive 60-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 117.667 and PAYEMS at 132843K as of 2010-07
regime_hint: synchronized supply-demand expansion with productivity gains
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of instances when both INDPRO and PAYEMS show month-over-month growth, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.12).
